narrative: OMX30 opens with tailwinds from broad European and Asian strength, particularly DAX's +0.40% and Asia's resilience despite Shanghai weakness. The index remains firmly in bull market territory at +8.2% above its 200-day moving average with MACD above signal and strong 90-day momentum (+5.2%), suggesting structural uptrend continuation. However, weak ADX (8.4) tempers conviction—this is a low-conviction bull setup requiring confirmation. Rising VIX (+2.94%) and higher US yields (4.453%, -0.04%) inject caution; copper's +2.91% and oil's +2.18% surge favor industrial names but risk-off undertones are emerging. Watch for US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET as the key catalyst.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens +0.3% to +0.5% on positive European and Asian close, with DAX momentum and copper strength driving industrial sentiment. Key technical: index sits in upper half of Bollinger bands (66% percentile) with clean breakout structure above 50-MA; support at 3085 (lower band), resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Sector rotation: Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Sandvik) benefit from stronger USD/SEK and copper rally; Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) neutral to slightly positive on stable yields. Red flag: ADX below 18 means reduce position sizing and trust trend breaks only on volume. Trade around US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) as volatility driver—VIX already 275bp elevated. Copper and oil strength is real; watch for any China slowdown concerns to reverse the move.
drivers: Strong global momentum: DAX +0.40%, Nikkei +0.91%, weighted correlation +0.295%
OMX30 structural strength: +8.2% above 200-MA, MACD above signal, ROC-90 +5.2%
USD strength (USD/SEK +0.17%) favors exporters; copper +2.91% supports industrials
VIX spike to 15.77 (+2.94%) creates near-term caution despite positive technicals

sectorImpacts.reason: USD/SEK +0.17% boosts export competitiveness; copper +2.91% supports demand signals; oil +2.18% partially offset by input cost pressure.
US 10Y yields stable at 4.453% (-0.04%); NIM expectations unchanged. Slight support from risk-on tone but no material impact.
Copper +2.91% is a direct revenue driver; gold -0.46% is minor headwind but copper momentum dominates.
NASDAQ +0.20% (weak) does not support tech rotation; Nvidia news is US-centric. Monitor for broader sentiment shifts.
EUR/SEK +0.12% weakens H&M's sourcing advantage slightly; oil +2.18% pressures consumer spending expectations.
Yields flat; no refinancing cost pressure. Remains in holding pattern.

watchList.reason: Copper +2.91% is a direct high-impact driver; momentum is constructive. Watch for 9500–9600 resistance breakout.
USD/SEK +0.17% and industrial sentiment from copper strength. Industrials are the primary bull case today.
USD strength and oil rally create mixed signals (export positive, input cost negative). Monitor for sector leadership confirmation.
EUR/SEK weakness and oil-driven consumer spending pressure. Watch for support breaks below recent lows.
Yields flat; no catalyst. Wait for central bank signals or material rate curve moves to trade.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish releases today. US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) is the key risk event—expect OMX30 to consolidate ahead of print. Previous: 233K; consensus expected around 231K.
topRisk: VIX spike (+2.94%) signals latent volatility; weak ADX (8.4) means trend is fragile. China slowdown fears (Shanghai -0.27%) could reverse commodity optimism fast. US Jobless Claims miss (higher readings) would trigger risk-off selloff across all risk assets.
technicalSummary: Bull market regime confirmed: +8.2% above 200-MA, +2.4% above 50-MA
MACD above signal with hist=+3.480 (bullish structure intact)
RSI-14 at 59.1 (neutral, no mean reversion risk)
ADX 8.4 (WEAK—reduce conviction, apply -30% magnitude discount per rules)
Bollinger %B=0.66 in upper half; width 6.18% (moderate volatility)
52-week: 89% of range, -2.8% from high (room to move either direction)
